member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalREmulatorResource
member.summary: Wraps an in a type that exposes container extension methods.
member.param: The inner resource used to store annotations.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalREmulatorResource.#ctor(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource)
member.summary: Wraps an in a type that exposes container extension methods.
member.param: The inner resource used to store annotations.

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalRServiceMode.Default
member.summary: In default mode, both the client and the hub server connect to SignalR Service and use the service as a proxy

member.name: F:Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalRServiceMode.Serverless
member.summary: In Serverless mode, SignalR Service works with Azure Functions to provide real time messaging capability. Azure Function uses Azure SignalR triggers and bindings to handle messages from the SignalR clients.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.AzureSignalRExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for adding the Azure SignalR resources to the application model.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureSignalRExtensions.AddAzureSignalR(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String)
member.summary: Adds an Azure SignalR resource to the application model.
member.param: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.
member.remarks:
By default references to the Azure SignalR resource will be assigned the following roles:
-
These can be replaced by calling .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureSignalRExtensions.AddAzureSignalR(Aspire.Hosting.IDistributedApplicationBuilder,System.String,Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalRServiceMode)
member.summary: Adds an Azure SignalR resource to the application model.
member.param: The name of the resource. This name will be used as the connection string name when referenced in a dependency.
The service mode of the resource.
member.remarks:
By default references to the Azure SignalR resource will be assigned the following roles:
-
Using additionally adds:
-
These can be replaced by calling .

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureSignalRExtensions.RunAsEmulator(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource},System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureSignalREmulatorResource}})
member.summary: Configures an Azure SignalR resource to be emulated. This resource requires an to be added to the application model. Please note that the resource will be emulated in Serverless mode.
member.remarks: This version of the package defaults to the tag of the / container image.
member.param: Callback that exposes underlying container used for emulation to allow for customization.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.AzureSignalRExtensions.WithRoleAssignments``1(Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{``0},Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.IResourceBuilder{Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource},Azure.Provisioning.SignalR.SignalRBuiltInRole[])
member.summary: Assigns the specified roles to the given resource, granting it the necessary permissions on the target Azure SignalR resource. This replaces the default role assignments for the resource.
member.param: The resource to which the specified roles will be assigned.
The built-in SignalR roles to be assigned.
member.returns: The updated with the applied role assignments.

member.name: T:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource
member.param: Callback to configure the Azure resources.

member.name: M:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.#ctor(System.String,System.Action{Aspire.Hosting.Azure.AzureResourceInfrastructure})
member.param: Callback to configure the Azure resources.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.IsEmulator
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the Azure SignalR resource is running in the local emulator.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.HostName
member.summary: Gets the "connectionString" output reference from the bicep template for Azure SignalR.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.NameOutputReference
member.summary: Gets the "name" output reference for the resource.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.UriExpression
member.summary: Gets the endpoint URI expression for the SignalR service.
member.remarks: In container mode (emulator), resolves to the container's endpoint URL. In Azure mode, resolves to the Azure SignalR service endpoint. Format: https://{hostname}.

member.name: P:Aspire.Hosting.ApplicationModel.AzureSignalRResource.ConnectionStringExpression
member.summary: Gets the connection string template for the manifest for Azure SignalR.
